title: Tephrosia nigrocalyx
intro: Tephrosia nigrocalyx is a plant species commonly known as the black calyx tephrosia or black-flowered pea tree, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting infections. The bioactive constituents of Tephrosia nigrocalyx include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
taxonomy: Tephrosia nigrocalyx is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This family includes a wide range of plants, many of which are economically important, such as beans, lentils, and peas. The genus Tephrosia itself contains over 250 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Tephrosia nigrocalyx is also supported by molecular studies, which have confirmed its placement within the Fabaceae family. Further research is needed to determine the specific relationships between Tephrosia nigrocalyx and other species within the genus.
uses: Tephrosia nigrocalyx, a plant native to tropical Africa, has been found to possess medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The plant is also said to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. In some regions, Tephrosia nigrocalyx is used as a treatment for snake bites and other venomous injuries. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of this plant.

side_effects: Tephrosia nigrocalyx is a plant species that has been reported to have various side effects due to its toxicity. Consuming the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to its alkaloids can also lead to liver damage and other systemic problems.
